---
title: "House_Price_Prediction"
author: "DANIAL ABEDI"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
#installing packeges
#install.packages(c("ggplot2","dplyr","scales"))
```




```{r}
#loading packages
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

```

```{r}
#loading data
train_data <- read.csv("F:/Projects GitHub/house-prices-prediction/data/train.csv")
dim(train_data)
```
```{r}
#visualizing target variable(SalesPrice)

ggplot(train_data,aes(x=SalePrice )) +
  geom_histogram(fill= "steelblue",color = "white",bins=75) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of House Price in Ames",
    substitle = "Most houses are clustered around $100k - $200k ",
    x = "Sales Price",
    Y = "Number of Houses"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(100000,200000,400000,600000,800000),
    labels = comma)
```
```{r}
#Creating new column with the logarithmic transformation of SalePrice

train_data <- train_data %>%
  mutate(Log_SalePrice=log(SalePrice))
ggplot(train_data, aes(Log_SalePrice))+
  geom_histogram(fill="seagreen",color="white",bins = 75)+
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = " Log-Transformed Distribution of House Price" ,
    subtitle = " Using logarithm so that machine understand it better",
    x="Log(SalePrice)",
    y="Number of Houses"
  )
```
